title: Mission to Zila

"'''Mission to Zila'''" is a short story written by [[Charlene Newcomb]] that was originally published in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 3|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 3]] by [[West End Games]] in August 1994 and was later archived online at [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] on [[September 3]], [[2004]].

==Plot summary==
[[Alexandra Winger|Alex Winger]] has moved from the [[Governor/Legends|governor's]] mansion to live at the [[University/Legends|university]], though she still visits her [[Parent/Legends|father]] from time to time. She serves as his [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] when he visits the nearby [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Zila]], and she recons the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] activity in the area. The [[Committee of Seven for Garosian Unification|underground Garosian resistance]] movement discovers that the Empire is [[Garrison/Legends|garrisoning]] Zila and building a [[Planet/Legends|planetary]] [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]] to better protect [[Garos IV]] from an inevitable [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] invasion. Guerrillas led by [[Chanceller|Chance]] strike at the Imperial supplies, destroying them before they can be put to use. 

==Continuity==
The chronological placement of ''Mission to Zila'' is not entirely clear; [[Alexandra Winger|Alex Winger]] is identified as being twenty years old, and [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] is said to have recently taken command of the Imperial military, both of which would suggest that the story takes place in [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]]; but [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] is said to be still in [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] hands, which would impose a date before [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]] (42:2:17) as stated in ''[[The Essential Atlas]]''. The reason for this is that ''Mission to Zila'' was written before ''[[X-Wing: Wedge's Gamble]]'', the source which fixed the [[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|liberation of Coruscant]] at that earlier date.

Perhaps the simplest [[Retroactive continuity|retcon]] is to suggest that the Imperials on [[Garos IV]] remained unaware of the loss of Coruscant, but that would not explain why the planet's well-organized [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] underground would be ignorant of such a major event more than two years; an alternative retcon would involve supposing that Thrawn had actually returned from the Unknown Regions much earlier than is generally supposed, but this would require some other means for explaining Alex's age.
